positive.prompt: Grill me on this architecture plan and record the clarified terminology and major decisions as we go
Stress-test this design by asking me dependency-aware questions before anyone implements it
Interrogate this proposal using terminal choice prompts and keep the working CONTEXT and ADRs local

negative.prompt: Turn this accepted specification into ordered implementation tasks
Review the current diff for correctness and security issues

evals.prompt: Grill me on a proposed multi-tenant billing design. Some questions depend on the tenancy model, while retention and invoice-export preferences are independent. Keep any working documentation local.
evals.expected_output: A dependency-aware interview that uses structured question batches where safe, records only resolved glossary terms and qualifying ADRs in local working documents, and stops at confirmed shared understanding
expectations: The agent inspects available repository evidence for facts and asks the user only for decisions
A dependent or high-risk decision is asked alone before questions whose meaning depends on it
Independent sibling decisions may be batched, with no more than three questions by default
The agent prefers one native structured-question call per batch and provides a numbered text fallback when unavailable
Every question has concrete alternatives, a recommended answer, and enough context to decide
The agent reconciles each answer batch and recomputes the decision tree before continuing
Working documents default to .agents/docs/grill-with-docs and remain local unless the user explicitly requests promotion
CONTEXT.md contains domain language rather than implementation details
An ADR is created only for a hard-to-reverse, surprising decision with a real trade-off
The agent asks for confirmation of shared understanding and does not implement or promote documents implicitly
